description: A semicolon is missing before the closing brace, causing a syntax error.
notes: Always end statements with a semicolon.
example: {'code': 'int main() { return 0 }', 'error_output': "error: expected ';' before '}' token"}

description: The compiler expected an expression but encountered an unexpected token.
notes: Check for missing expressions or incorrect operator usage.
example: {'code': 'int main() { if (x) else y; }', 'error_output': "error: expected primary-expression before 'else' token"}

description: A character or string literal is missing its closing quote.
notes: Always close string and character literals with matching quotes.
example: {'code': 'int main() { char* s = "hello; return 0; }', 'error_output': 'error: missing terminating " character'}

description: An invalid character was found in the source code.
notes: Remove invalid characters or check for encoding issues.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int x@ = 5; return 0; }', 'error_output': "error: stray '@' in program"}

message_pattern: '%s' was not declared in this scope
description: Identifier used without declaration or outside its scope.
notes: Declare the function or variable before using it.
example: {'code': 'int main() { foo(); }', 'error_output': "error: 'foo' was not declared in this scope"}

message_pattern: return-statement with no value, in function returning '%s'
description: A return statement without a value in a function that expects a return value.
notes: Add a return value or change the function return type to void.
example: {'code': 'int foo() { return; }', 'error_output': "error: return-statement with no value, in function returning 'int'"}

message_pattern: void value not ignored as it ought to be
description: Trying to use the result of a void function as a value.
notes: Don't assign void functions to variables or use them in expressions.
example: {'code': 'void foo() {} int main() { int x = foo(); }', 'error_output': 'error: void value not ignored as it ought to be'}

description: Attempting to convert between incompatible types.
notes: Use explicit casts or ensure type compatibility.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int* p = 10; }', 'error_output': "error: invalid conversion from 'int' to 'int*'"}

description: Type mismatch during variable initialization.
notes: Ensure the initializer type matches the variable type.
example: {'code': 'int main() { double d = "hello"; }', 'error_output': "error: cannot convert 'const char*' to 'double' in initialization"}

message_pattern: invalid operands of types '%s' and '%s' to binary '%s'
description: Using incompatible types with a binary operator.
notes: Overload the operator or use compatible types.
example: {'code': 'struct S{}; int main() { S s1, s2; return s1 + s2; }', 'error_output': "error: invalid operands of types 'S' and 'S' to binary 'operator+'"}

message_pattern: request for member '%s' in '%s', which is of non-class type '%s'
description: Trying to access a member of a non-class type.
notes: Only class/struct types have members.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int x; return x.size(); }', 'error_output': "error: request for member 'size' in 'x', which is of non-class type 'int'"}

description: The linker cannot find the definition of a declared function or variable.
notes: Provide the definition or link the required library.
example: {'code': '// main.cpp\nvoid foo();\nint main() { foo(); return 0; }', 'error_output': "error: undefined reference to 'foo'"}

description: The same symbol is defined in multiple translation units.
notes: Use 'inline' for functions or 'extern' for variables with one definition.

message_pattern: no matching function for call to '%s'
description: No viable template function overload matches the arguments.
notes: Provide the correct arguments or add missing template parameters.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> void foo(T t) {}\nint main() { foo(); }', 'error_output': "error: no matching function for call to 'foo()'"}

description: The compiler cannot deduce template arguments from function arguments.
notes: Check template parameter requirements and argument types.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> void foo(T* t) {}\nint main() { foo(5); }', 'error_output': "error: no matching function for call to 'foo(int)'\nnote: template argument deduction/substitution failed"}

description: Missing template parameter in template specialization or instantiation.
notes: Provide template arguments when using template classes.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> class A {};\nint main() { A a; }', 'error_output': "error: invalid use of template-name 'A' without an argument list"}

message_pattern: macro '%s' requires %s arguments, but only %s given
description: Incorrect number of arguments passed to a macro.
notes: Check the macro definition for required arguments.
example: {'code': '#define ADD(x, y) ((x) + (y))\nint main() { return ADD(5); }', 'error_output': 'error: macro "ADD" requires 2 arguments, but only 1 given'}

message_pattern: missing terminating %s character in macro invocation
description: Unclosed parenthesis in macro invocation.
notes: Ensure all macro arguments are properly enclosed.
example: {'code': '#define FOO(x) x\nint main() { FOO(5; return 0; }', 'error_output': "error: missing terminating ')' character in macro invocation"}

description: Syntax error where an identifier was expected but another token found.
notes: Variable names cannot start with numbers.
example: {'code': 'int 123var = 5;', 'error_output': 'error: expected unqualified-id before numeric constant'}

message_pattern: dependent-name '%s' is parsed as a non-type, but instantiation yields a type
description: Template dependent name resolution ambiguity.
notes: Use 'typename' keyword for dependent types: 'typename T::x y;'
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> void foo() { T::x y; }', 'error_output': "error: dependent-name 'T::x' is parsed as a non-type, but instantiation yields a type"}

message_pattern: ISO C++ forbids declaration of '%s' with no type
description: Missing type in variable declaration, often caused by macro issues.
notes: Ensure macros expand to complete declarations.
example: {'code': '#define VAR x\nint main() { VAR = 5; }', 'error_output': "error: ISO C++ forbids declaration of 'VAR' with no type"}

description: No main function found in the program.
notes: Every executable must have exactly one main function.
example: {'code': 'void foo() {}', 'error_output': "error: undefined reference to `main'"}

description: The same entity is defined multiple times.
notes: Use 'extern' for declarations and define only once.
example: {'code': 'int x = 5;\nint x = 10;', 'error_output': "error: redefinition of 'x'"}

message_pattern: cannot bind non-const lvalue reference of type '%s' to an rvalue of type '%s'
description: Trying to bind a non-const lvalue reference to a temporary.
notes: Use const reference or pass by value.
example: {'code': 'void foo(int& x) {}\nint main() { foo(5); }', 'error_output': "error: cannot bind non-const lvalue reference of type 'int&' to an rvalue of type 'int'"}

message_pattern: template instantiation depth exceeds maximum of %s
description: Excessive template recursion depth.
notes: Reduce recursion depth or use iterative solutions.
example: {'code': 'template<int N> struct Fact { enum { value = N * Fact<N-1>::value }; };\ntemplate<> struct Fact<0> { enum { value = 1 }; };\nint main() { return Fact<1000>::value; }', 'error_output': 'error: template instantiation depth exceeds maximum of 900'}

description: Losing const qualifier when passing arguments.
notes: Add const to parameter type or remove const from argument.
example: {'code': 'void foo(int* p) {}\nint main() { const int* p; foo(p); }', 'error_output': "error: invalid conversion from 'const int*' to 'int*'"}

message_pattern: invalid static_cast from type '%s' to type '%s'
description: Invalid static cast between incompatible types.
notes: static_cast requires related types or well-defined conversions.
example: {'code': 'int main() { double d = 1.0; int* p = static_cast<int*>(&d); }', 'error_output': "error: invalid static_cast from type 'double*' to type 'int*'"}

message_pattern: type '%s' is not a direct base of '%s'
description: Invalid base class in static_cast or dynamic_cast.
notes: Classes must be related by inheritance for such casts.
example: {'code': 'class A {}; class B {};\nint main() { A a; B* b = static_cast<B*>(&a); }', 'error_output': "error: invalid static_cast from type 'A*' to type 'B*'"}

description: Missing expression in macro or conditional.
notes: Macros in #if must expand to valid expressions.
example: {'code': '#define COND\nint main() { #if COND return 1; #endif }', 'error_output': "error: expected expression before ';' token"}

description: static_assert condition must be a constant expression.
notes: Use constant expressions in static_assert.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int x = 5; static_assert(x == 5); }', 'error_output': 'error: non-constant condition for static assertion'}

description: Functions cannot return other functions directly.
notes: Return function pointers instead: int(*)()
example: {'code': 'int() foo() { return nullptr; }', 'error_output': "error: function cannot return function type 'int()'"}

description: Template specialization inside a class or function.
notes: Move specializations to namespace scope.
example: {'code': 'class C { template<typename T> void foo(); template<> void foo<int>(); };', 'error_output': 'error: explicit specialization in non-namespace scope'}

description: Attempting to use a deleted function.
notes: Deleted functions cannot be called.
example: {'code': 'void foo() = delete;\nint main() { foo(); }', 'error_output': "error: use of deleted function 'void foo()'"}

description: Using a type that has been declared but not defined.
notes: Provide complete type definition before use.
example: {'code': 'class A;\nint main() { A a; }', 'error_output': "error: aggregate 'A a' has incomplete type and cannot be defined"}

description: Virtual function table missing for polymorphic class.
notes: Define all virtual functions or make them pure virtual.
example: {'code': 'class Base { virtual void foo(); };\nint main() { Base b; }', 'error_output': "error: undefined reference to 'vtable for Base'"}

description: Template specialization appears after first use.
notes: Declare specializations before first use.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> void foo() {}\nvoid bar() { foo<int>(); }\ntemplate<> void foo<int>() {}', 'error_output': "error: specialization of 'void foo()' after instantiation"}

message_pattern: call to implicitly-deleted default constructor of '%s'
description: Default constructor is implicitly deleted.
notes: Provide constructor or initialize const/reference members.
example: {'code': 'class A { const int x; };\nint main() { A a; }', 'error_output': "error: call to implicitly-deleted default constructor of 'A'"}

message_pattern: binding reference of type '%s' to '%s' discards qualifiers
description: Losing const qualifier in reference binding.
notes: Use const reference: const int& y = x;
example: {'code': 'int main() { const int x = 5; int& y = x; }', 'error_output': "error: binding reference of type 'int&' to 'const int' discards qualifiers"}

message_pattern: too many arguments provided to function-like macro invocation
description: Extra arguments in macro call.
notes: Match macro call arguments to macro definition.
example: {'code': '#define FOO(x) x\nint main() { FOO(1, 2); }', 'error_output': 'error: too many arguments provided to function-like macro invocation'}

message_pattern: type/value mismatch at argument %d in template parameter list for '%s'
description: Wrong kind of template argument (type vs non-type).
notes: Use correct template argument types.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> class A {};\nint main() { A<5> a; }', 'error_output': "error: type/value mismatch at argument 1 in template parameter list for 'template<class T> class A'"}

description: Attempting to call a deleted function.
notes: Deleted functions cannot be used.
example: {'code': 'struct A { A() = delete; };\nint main() { A a; }', 'error_output': "error: use of deleted function 'A::A()'"}

message_pattern: cannot dynamic_cast '%s' (of type '%s') to type '%s' (source is not a pointer or reference)
description: Invalid use of dynamic_cast.
notes: dynamic_cast requires pointers or references to polymorphic types.
example: {'code': 'class Base {};\nint main() { Base b; dynamic_cast<Base>(b); }', 'error_output': "error: cannot dynamic_cast 'b' (of type 'Base') to type 'Base' (target is not a pointer or reference)"}

description: Various template parameter errors.
notes: Match template parameter kinds in declarations and definitions.
example: {'code': 'template<int N> struct S {};\ntemplate<typename T> void foo(S<T> s) {}', 'error_output': "error: template parameter 'T'"}

description: Multiple declarations with different types.
notes: Function declarations must have consistent types.
example: {'code': 'int foo(); double foo();', 'error_output': "error: conflicting declaration 'double foo()'"}

message_pattern: invalid cast from type '%s' to type '%s'
description: General invalid cast error.
notes: Use appropriate cast for the conversion.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int x = reinterpret_cast<int>("hello"); }', 'error_output': "error: invalid cast from type 'const char*' to type 'int'"}

description: Missing definition for declared entity.
notes: Provide definition in some translation unit.
example: {'code': 'extern int global;\nint main() { return global; }', 'error_output': "error: undefined reference to `global'"}

message_pattern: no match for '%s' (operand types are '%s' and '%s')
description: No matching operator for given operand types.
notes: Define the operator or use compatible types.
example: {'code': 'struct S{};\nint main() { S s; return s + s; }', 'error_output': "error: no match for 'operator+' (operand types are 'S' and 'S')"}

message_pattern: template argument for '%s' is not a constant expression
description: Non-constant expression used as template argument.
notes: Template arguments must be constant expressions.
example: {'code': 'template<int N> struct S {};\nint main() { int x = 5; S<x> s; }', 'error_output': "error: the value of 'x' is not usable in a constant expression"}

description: Missing identifier in macro or declaration.
notes: Macros should not conflict with variable names.
example: {'code': '#define CONST 5\nint main() { int CONST = 10; }', 'error_output': 'error: expected identifier before numeric constant'}

message_pattern: cannot call member function '%s' without object
description: Calling non-static member function without object.
notes: Call member functions on objects: s.foo();
example: {'code': 'struct S { void foo() {} };\nint main() { S::foo(); }', 'error_output': "error: cannot call member function 'void S::foo()' without object"}

message_pattern: invalid const_cast from type '%s' to type '%s'
description: Invalid use of const_cast.
notes: const_cast can only add/remove const/volatile qualifiers.
example: {'code': 'int main() { int x = 5; const_cast<float>(x); }', 'error_output': "error: invalid const_cast from type 'int' to type 'float'"}

description: Multiple template specializations match equally well.
notes: Remove ambiguous specializations.
example: {'code': 'template<typename T> void foo(T) {}\ntemplate<> void foo(int*) {}\ntemplate<> void foo(int*) {}', 'error_output': "error: ambiguous template specialization 'foo<int>'"}

description: Missing class name in inheritance or declaration.
notes: Provide class name in declaration.
example: {'code': 'class : public Base {};', 'error_output': "error: expected class-name before '{' token"}

description: Array of incomplete type.
notes: Complete type definition required for arrays.
example: {'code': 'class A;\nint main() { A arr[5]; }', 'error_output': "error: array has incomplete element type 'A'"}

description: Missing definition of class member.
notes: Define all class member functions.
example: {'code': 'class C { public: void foo(); };\nint main() { C c; c.foo(); }', 'error_output': "error: undefined reference to `C::foo()'"}
